Paper Title: The integration of CAD/CAM and Rapid Prototyping in Product Development: A review
Authors: A.K. Matta, D. Ranga Raju, K.N.S. Suman
Problem Statement: Due to international competition, industries need to reduce time to market and cost while assuring high quality, requiring better CAD/CAM and RP integration.
Methodology: Literature review of recent research on CAD/CAM RP&M systems, detailing key issues like STEP/XML integration, modelling and algorithms.
Future Work Suggestions: Outlook on future development of CAD/CAM RP&M systems, integrating cloud technologies and remote manufacturing.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Contextual Overview. Provides older perspective on CAD/CAM integration with Rapid Prototyping. Gap: Lacks modern AI/ML approaches and focuses more on additive manufacturing than CNC automation.

Paper Title: Machine Learning approach for Predictive Maintenance in Industry 4.0
Authors: Marina Paolanti, Luca Romeo, Andrea Felicetti, Adriano Mancini, Emanuele Frontoni, Jelena Loncarski
Problem Statement: Unscheduled downtime and lack of reliable fault forecasting in electric motors/cutting machines lead to severe economic losses.
Methodology: Data-driven approach collecting data from sensors and PLCs via MQTT/MES, and training a Random Forest model on Azure Machine Learning Studio to predict machine states.
Future Work Suggestions: Implement deeper diagnostic tracking and predictive maintenance across more varied datasets.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Relevant baseline. Showcases cloud-based RF implementation on cutting machines. Gap: Uses a standard ML algorithm without addressing complex sequence modeling or deep learning integration.

Paper Title: Thirty years of the International Journal of Computer Integrated Manufacturing: a bibliometric analysis
Authors: Sigifredo Laengle, Nikunja Mohan Modak, José M. Merigó, Catalina De La Sotta
Problem Statement: Analyzing the growth, impact, and thematic trends of computer integrated manufacturing (CIM) technologies over three decades to identify future research directions.
Methodology: Bibliometric analysis of publications in IJCIM from 1988 to 2017 using Web of Science Core Collection and VOS viewer software for visual mapping of keywords and co-authorship.
Future Work Suggestions: Guide editorial policies and point researchers toward trending topics like interoperability, cloud manufacturing, and machine learning in CIM.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): High-level theoretical review. Validates the importance of CAD/CAM integration and predictive maintenance. Gap: Bibliometric analysis without proposing a specific technical architecture.

Paper Title: An Automated Method Mapping Parametric Features Between Computer Aided Design Software
Authors: Toby David Borland
Problem Statement: Translating CAD models while retaining parametric design features is limited by structural and semantic heterogeneity between vendor CAD programs.
Methodology: Combined semantic text matching and 3D surface intersection geometric queries. Developed a Genetic Algorithm to map equivalent function parameters across heterogeneous CAD API libraries.
Future Work Suggestions: Refactoring algorithms into faster languages and optimizing sampling techniques to reduce direct calls made to the CAD API via the COM interface.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Foundational. Validates the complexity of CAD API bridging and AFR. Gap: Highlights the difficulty of inter-CAD communication, which our web-based standardized architecture could bypass entirely.

Paper Title: STEP-NC-based machining architecture applied to industrial robots
Authors: Juan S. Toquica, Sasa Zivanovic, Renan Bonnard, Efrain Rodriguez, Alberto J. Alvares, João C. E. Ferreira
Problem Statement: Industrial robots are usually equipped with specific controller languages that hinder shop floor adoption and limit interoperability within the manufacturing digital chain.
Methodology: Proposed an ISO 10303-238 (AP-238) compliant architecture using Mastercam for programming, STEP-NC Machine for simulation, and execution on two retrofitted industrial robots via an open LinuxCNC controller.
Future Work Suggestions: Development of a robotics solution with open or proprietary control systems that can directly read and execute STEP-NC programs, and integrating part inspection data feedback for closed-loop manufacturing.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Demonstrates seamless integration of CAD/CAM and CNC using STEP-NC over the internet. Gap: Still relies on translating paths to G-code via LinuxCNC instead of direct STEP-NC execution at the controller level, and lacks closed-loop feedback mechanisms.

Paper Title: A hybrid predictive maintenance approach for CNC machine tool driven by Digital Twin
Authors: Weichao Luo, Tianliang Hu, Yingxin Ye, Chengrui Zhang, Yongli Wei
Problem Statement: Traditional predictive maintenance methods fail to consider the status variety and consistency of CNC machine tools over their life cycle, leading to inaccurate and untimely fault predictions.
Methodology: Proposed a Digital Twin (DT) model-based and data-driven hybrid approach. The framework merges physical mathematical models with real-time operational sensing data to conduct fault forecasts.
Future Work Suggestions: Future implementation of DT models based on cloud and edge computing, as well as applying model migration learning based on DT.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Fits perfectly with the remote process monitoring and predictive maintenance pillars. Gap: Architecture focuses heavily on the twin, leaving an opening for a more lightweight, direct API-to-cloud predictive integration.

Paper Title: Machine Learning in Predictive Maintenance towards Sustainable Smart Manufacturing in Industry 4.0
Authors: Zeki Murat Çınar, Abubakar Abdussalam Nuhu, Qasim Zeeshan, Orhan Korhan, Mohammed Asmael, Babak Safaei
Problem Statement: The difficulty of selecting appropriate ML techniques, data sizes, and equipment types for PdM applications causes time loss and infeasible maintenance scheduling.
Methodology: Systematic literature review utilizing the PRISMA framework to categorize 126 articles by ML technique, equipment, device, and data type.
Future Work Suggestions: Offers guidelines and foundation for further research into specific ML algorithm selections based on structured taxonomy.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Contextual Overview. Maps out the entire landscape of PdM using ML up to 2020. Gap: Serves as a theoretical foundation rather than an architectural blueprint.

Paper Title: CAD-CAM Technology: A literature Review
Authors: Mohammed A. Abdulla, Hala Khuder Ali, Raghad S Jamel
Problem Statement: Need to review the application, designing, and manufacturing via CAD/CAM in prosthodontics/dental restorations as it developed rapidly over the last two decades.
Methodology: Literature review of the components of dental CAD/CAM systems including optical/mechanical scanning, 3D design software, and subtractive (3-axis to 5-axis milling) or additive manufacturing phases.
Future Work Suggestions: Further enhancement in intraoral scanning and materials for milled dental restorations.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Tangentially relevant. Highlights specific CAD/CAM CNC application (dentistry). Gap: Domain-specific to dentistry and lacks general mechanical engineering AI/ML integration.

Paper Title: Present and Future Impacts of Computer-Aided Design/ Computer-Aided Manufacturing (CAD/CAM)
Authors: Peter P. Ikubanni, Adekunle A. Adeleke, Olayinka O. Agboola, Chiebuka T. Christopher, Boluwatife S. Ademola, Joseph Okonkwo, Olanrewaju S. Adesina, Peter O. Omoniyi, Esther T. Akinlabi
Problem Statement: The need to examine the present impacts of CAD/CAM on sectors like architecture, manufacturing, and aerospace, as well as its future potential to ease production against traditional methods.
Methodology: A comprehensive review examining current applications and exploring future trends including 3-D bioprinting, virtual reality, artificial intelligence, cloud technology, and the industrial internet of things (IIoT).
Future Work Suggestions: More research focus on biomaterials for 3-D bioprinting for tissue engineering applications, and generating numerous models from different laboratories.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Contextual Overview. Provides a broad understanding of CAD/CAM's future, including Cloud and IIoT integration. Gap: It is a broad literature review without a specific technical architecture or empirical implementation.

Paper Title: Optimizing Predictive Maintenance Strategies for CNC Machining Centers: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Algorithms
Authors: Dr. Gorakh Wakhare
Problem Statement: Assess the performance, robustness, and interpretability of ML models used in predictive maintenance, addressing challenges related to data interoperability and model interpretability.
Methodology: Mixed-methods approach combining quantitative analysis of ML model performance (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Neural Network) and qualitative insights from operator surveys regarding Explainable AI.
Future Work Suggestions: Cross-industry collaboration, scalability planning, and deeper exploration of specific XAI techniques across diverse manufacturing environments.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Relevant. Addresses interpretability and robustness across operational conditions. Gap: Limited exploration of specific XAI techniques and contextual specificity to only CNC centers.

Paper Title: STEP-NC AP238 - an excellent paradigm for smart manufacturing
Authors: Deepanshu Srivastava, Venkateswara Rao Komma
Problem Statement: G-M codes are low level and lack product geometry and process information, becoming a bottleneck for seamless integration of CAD/CAPP/CAM in Industry 4.0.
Methodology: Review of STEP-NC standard (specifically ISO 10303-238 / AP238) which provides a rich set of information by using EXPRESS language for feature-based process planning.
Future Work Suggestions: Utilizing STEP-NC AP238 for seamless integration of CAD/CAM and CNC, forming an intelligent manufacturing environment for Industry 4.0.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Explains why STEP-NC AP238 is superior to G-Code for automated manufacturing. Gap: A review paper rather than presenting an empirical framework or AI integration.

Paper Title: The breadth of Industry 4.0 technologies: factory level dynamic capabilities as antecedents and competitive advantage as outcome
Authors: Hamed Nayernia
Problem Statement: There is a lack of understanding regarding the capabilities needed to integrate and manage a breadth of Industry 4.0 technologies at the factory level and their impact on operational performance.
Methodology: Mixed-method approach combining a systematic literature review, semi-structured interviews, and a cross-sectional survey of 320 UK factories analyzed using structural equation modelling (SEM).
Future Work Suggestions: Explore the balance of open innovation, R&D, and long-term relational governance during 14.0 adoption, alongside human resource impacts.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): High-level operational strategy context. Validates the need for design-manufacturing integration (CAD/CAM). Gap: Business/management focus rather than a mechanical engineering CAD/CNC API architecture.

Paper Title: Research on the Semantic Model of R&D and Design Resources of Group Enterprises
Authors: Lei WANG, Zhengang ZHAO, Nan ZHAO, LI Li, Yimin HUANG, Jiangtao Huang, Qingpeng Wang
Problem Statement: R&D and design resources in group manufacturing enterprises are scattered, isolated, and have low sharing degree, hindering collaborative product innovation.
Methodology: Established an R&D design resource information model using object-oriented and semantic knowledge concepts to describe cloud manufacturing software resources. Used TF-IDF for attribute extraction of enterprise software resource text.
Future Work Suggestions: Enhancing semantic attribute extraction efficiency from unstructured CAD resource texts to support cloud-based product innovation.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Relevant to the cloud-platform/database side of CAD resources. Gap: Focuses heavily on resource taxonomy and semantics rather than the mechanical automation of CAD-to-CNC workflows.

Paper Title: A review of automated cutting tool selection methods
Authors: Gowthri Navaneethan, Suresh Palanisamy, Prem Prakash Jayaraman, Yong-Bin Kang, Guy Stephens, Angelo Papageorgiou, John Navarro
Problem Statement: Manual cutting tool selection is time-consuming, prone to errors, and requires expert knowledge, driving the need for automated CT selection in complex machining.
Methodology: Comprehensive review of automated cutting tool selection methods, analyzing the inputs, outputs, and various AI techniques (like expert systems, machine learning, and genetic algorithms) used.
Future Work Suggestions: Developing comprehensive case management systems, automating data capturing for sustainability factors, and utilizing manufacturer configurations for custom tool selection.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Relevant to process automation. Emphasizes AI/ML for tool selection. Gap: Lacks a concrete unified architecture combining tool selection with direct CNC control and remote monitoring.

Paper Title: Predictive Maintenance of CNC Machines Using Integrated Risk Assessment and Deep Learning Forecasting
Authors: Huseyin Ali Sarikaya
Problem Statement: SMEs struggle to implement predictive maintenance due to a lack of digitization. A systematic method combining risk prioritization and deep learning forecasting is needed to reduce CNC downtime.
Methodology: Analyzed 2 years of CNC failure data. Applied Fine-Kinney risk assessment and TOPSIS to prioritize failures. Developed RNN, LSTM, and GRU models in Python to forecast time to next failure.
Future Work Suggestions: Integrating the dual-stage risk prioritization with real-time sensor streams and exploring Transformer-based models or Explainable AI (XAI) for enhanced prediction trust.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Combines maintenance risk assessment with LSTM/GRU forecasting for CNCs. Gap: Relies on historical failure logs rather than high-frequency real-time sensor data integration.

Paper Title: CAM as a Service with dynamic toolpath generation ability for process optimization in STEP-NC compliant CNC machining
Authors: Kaiyao Zhang, Wenlei Xiao, Xiangming Fan, Gang Zhao
Problem Statement: Current calculation methods of toolpath lengths in process optimization lack flexibility. Toolpaths need to be generated automatically based on dynamic combinations of process parameters rather than fixed features.
Methodology: Deployed CAM on the cloud using a STEP-NC edge-cloud collaborative system. Proposed a non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm III with CAM as a service (NSGAIII-CaaS) to automatically generate toolpaths interacting with parameter optimization.
Future Work Suggestions: Further research into applying various cloud service technologies and integrating real machining signals to achieve smarter optimization of milling parameters.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Addresses the exact bottleneck of web-based CAD/CAM architectures. Gap: The paper relies on CAM services that still require human experience for machining strategies; true automation via AI is missing.

Paper Title: Predictive Maintenance in CNC Machines Using Machine Learning
Authors: N. Arjun, Dr. Karthikeyan G, G.Haribabu, Vennila V, Sarfaraz Ahmed A, S.Arulraj
Problem Statement: Existing PdM models suffer from high computing costs, lack of interpretability, and inflexibility in embedded CNC systems.
Methodology: A unified hybrid PdM pipeline combining multimodal sensor data (vibration, temperature, acoustic) with Random Forest for classification and LSTM for RUL prediction, utilizing LIME for explainable AI.
Future Work Suggestions: Generalization to different machine configurations, adaptive transfer learning, federated learning approaches, and augmented reality interfaces.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): State-of-the-art. Combines edge-computation, XAI, and multimodal data. Gap: Relies on a single CNC platform and does not fully model environmental noise variation.

Paper Title: An Industrial IoT Framework for Predictive Maintenance of CNC Lathe Spindles: Integrating Deep Learning and Cloud-Based Analytics
Authors: Nikhil M. Thoppil, V. Vasu
Problem Statement: Existing predictive maintenance models are restricted to offline analysis or lack the integration needed for end-to-end real-time predictive maintenance in industrial settings.
Methodology: Developed an IIoT framework using an accelerated run-to-failure testbed for a CNC lathe spindle. Employed NCA for feature selection and trained LSTM and bi-LSTM models for RUL estimation, implemented on the ThingSpeak IoT platform.
Future Work Suggestions: Extending the modular architecture to various machine components and scaling across diverse industrial setups.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. State-of-the-art implementation of cloud-based predictive maintenance for CNC using LSTM and IIoT. Gap: Narrowly focused on spindle vibration rather than full CAD-to-CNC workflow integration.

Paper Title: Predictive maintenance system for CNC machines
Authors: Sridevi S, R Ramya, Jayajevaa N, Nitin Sriram M, Deeksha S R, Jacob M
Problem Statement: Traditional reactive and preventive maintenance approaches for CNC machines are inefficient, causing unexpected downtime and resource misallocation.
Methodology: Developed "Be Shield", a preventive maintenance system integrating a sensor kit (Arduino, temperature, vibration, humidity) with Python-based Random Forest Machine Learning models for real-time anomaly detection.
Future Work Suggestions: Extending from classification models (likelihood of failure) to regression-based models (like Gradient Boosting) for precise Remaining Useful Life (RUL) estimation.
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Provides a concrete lightweight architecture (Arduino + Python + ML) for CNC predictive maintenance. Gap: Focuses on basic ML (Random Forest) without deep learning sequence modeling.

Paper Title: Research on Predictive Maintenance of CNC Machine Tools Based on Deep Learning
Authors: Jing Pu
Problem Statement: Traditional statistical and shallow machine learning models struggle to model long-term dependencies and require manual feature engineering, making it difficult to capture the complex, nonlinear degradation patterns of CNC machine tools.
Methodology: Developed a hybrid deep learning framework integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for spatial feature extraction and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to model temporal sequences, utilizing multi-sensor data fusion (vibration, acoustic emission, current, temperature).
Future Work Suggestions: Explore sophisticated data augmentation, semi-supervised learning, transfer learning, and methods for model interpretability (attention mechanisms/saliency maps).
Critical Summary (Relevance & Gaps): Highly Relevant. Proposes a state-of-the-art CNN-LSTM framework for CNC predictive maintenance using multi-modal sensor fusion. Gap: Acknowledges a "black-box" nature lacking interpretability in safety-critical applications, which could be addressed through XAI techniques.
